title: Fix rate limiter token bucket simulation to track actual tokens
description: PatternBehavioralSimulator.tsx:166-176 calculates remaining tokens as `max(0, bucketSize - (totalRequests + i) * cost + floor((totalRequests + i) / 5) * refillRate)`. This formula doesn't cap at bucketSize and uses cumulative request count instead of tracking actual token state. At 25 requests with bucket=20/cost=1/refillRate=10, yields 45 tokens (exceeds capacity). Root cause: simulation lacks independent token state variable. Fix: add `tokens` field to SimMetrics, refill per tick capped at bucketSize, deduct per request.
acceptanceCriteria: Token count never exceeds bucketSize at any simulation step
Tokens refill by refillRate per tick, clamped to bucketSize
Each request deducts costPerReq tokens; insufficient tokens = rejected
Metrics display shows current token count
'Within Limit' scenario has near-zero rejections; 'Burst Traffic' drains bucket visibly

title: Unify LLD problem data into single canonical source
description: Two separate LLD problem datasets: lib/lld/problems.ts (10 problems, LLDProblem type with starterClasses/starterRelationships/hints) and lib/seo/lld-problems-data.ts (25 problems, different schema with slug/title/category/keyPatterns/interviewFrequency). Module uses one, SEO pages use the other. Confusing and unsynchronized. Unify into single source in lib/lld/problems.ts with full schema covering both needs. SEO data can derive from unified source.
acceptanceCriteria: Single canonical LLD_PROBLEMS array contains all 25 problems
Each problem has both starter diagram data AND SEO fields (slug, category, keyPatterns)
lib/seo/lld-problems-data.ts imports from canonical source or is removed
LLD module sidebar shows all 25 problems
SEO pages continue to work with correct metadata
No duplicate problem definitions remain

title: Add getter/setter and decorator support to code-to-diagram parser
description: code-to-diagram.ts:248-275 parseTsMethods regex `methodRe` expects `name(params): returnType` but misses TypeScript getters (`get name(): string { }`) and setters (`set name(val: string) { }`). parsePyMethods at line 458-480 misses @property decorated methods and @staticmethod. These are common in real-world code users would paste. Fix: extend regexes to handle get/set keywords and Python decorator forms.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript getter `get name(): string` parsed as attribute with '+' visibility
TypeScript setter `set name(val: string)` parsed as method
Python @property methods parsed as attributes
Python @staticmethod parsed as methods with correct visibility
Existing parsing of normal methods unchanged
Unit tests added for each new case

title: Add localStorage persistence for diagram state
description: useLLDModule hook at LLDModule.tsx:3206-3459 uses 12 useState hooks with no persistence. Refreshing loses all state. Add auto-save to localStorage for: current sidebar mode, active item ID, classes/relationships array, selected class/message/state IDs. Load on mount. Clear on explicit user action. Use a version key to handle schema changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagram state auto-saves to localStorage on every change
Page refresh restores exact previous diagram state
Sidebar mode and active selection restored
Works correctly with user-added classes and custom positions
Clear/Reset button removes saved state
Schema versioning prevents crashes on data format changes

title: Improve parse error messages with specific failure context
description: LLDModule.tsx:1024-1026 catch block shows generic 'Failed to parse code. Check syntax and try again.' with no detail about what the parser found or where it got confused. Enhance to show: number of classes found, number of relationships extracted, and specific parsing warnings (e.g., 'Found class Dog but could not find parent Animal in the code').
acceptanceCriteria: Successful parse with warnings shows 'Found N classes, M relationships. Warnings: ...'
Failed parse shows what was partially parsed before failure
Unresolved relationship targets (e.g., parent class not found) reported as warnings, not errors
Error message under 3 lines, actionable

title: Add input size guard to code-to-diagram textarea
description: LLDModule.tsx:1075 code textarea has no maxLength. A user could paste megabytes of code, causing the regex parser at code-to-diagram.ts:41-138 to freeze the browser. Add maxLength=102400 (100KB) to textarea, display warning if input exceeds 50KB, and cap parsed classes at 100 with a message.
acceptanceCriteria: Textarea has maxLength of 100KB
Warning shown if input exceeds 50KB: 'Large input may be slow to parse'
Parser caps output at 100 classes with message: 'Showing first 100 of N classes'
Browser does not freeze on large paste operations

title: Add search and filter to sidebar pattern and problem lists
description: PatternBrowser (LLDModule.tsx:543-601) shows 15 patterns across 4 categories with no search. ProblemsBrowser (717-751) shows 10 problems with no filter. As content grows (25+ problems, potentially more patterns), browsing becomes tedious. Add search input at top of sidebar content area that filters across all modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Search input appears at top of sidebar content area
Typing filters visible items across current mode (patterns/problems/etc.)
Search matches name, description, and category
Empty results show 'No matches for [query]'
Clearing search restores full list
Search persists when switching between sidebar tabs

title: Increase sub-11px SVG font sizes to minimum 11px
description: Six SVG text elements use font sizes below 11px: stereotype label at LLDModule.tsx:362 (9px), relationship label at line 502 (10px), cardinality text at line 516 (9px), category count at line 574 (10px), sequence order at line 2238 (9px), state machine transition at line 2761 (9px). Below WCAG readability guidelines. Increase all to minimum 11px, adjusting layout as needed.
acceptanceCriteria: No SVG text element has fontSize below 11px
All text remains visually balanced within its context
Cardinality labels don't overlap class edges at new size
Transition labels fit within background rects

title: Remove dead LLDModule component export
description: LLDModule.tsx:3461-3463 exports `const LLDModule = memo(function LLDModule() { return null; })` — a component that renders nothing. The module works via the useLLDModule hook only. This export is confusing for developers. Remove it or, if needed for module registry, add a comment explaining why it returns null.
acceptanceCriteria: Dead LLDModule component removed or documented
No imports of LLDModule component break
Module registry (if any) updated to use hook pattern

title: Add unit tests for code-to-diagram parser edge cases
description: code-to-diagram.ts (496 lines) has no dedicated test file. It parses user-provided TypeScript and Python code — the most security-sensitive and error-prone path in the module. Missing tests for: getters/setters, nested generics (Map<string, List<int>>), empty classes, single-line classes, deeply nested inheritance, enum with explicit values, abstract method without body, multiline method signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created at src/lib/lld/__tests__/code-to-diagram.test.ts
Tests cover: empty class, getter/setter, nested generics, enum with values
Tests cover: abstract method, multiline params, deeply nested inheritance chain
Tests cover: Python @property, @staticmethod, multiple inheritance
Tests cover: invalid input (empty string, just comments, HTML)
All tests pass on current implementation

title: Add unit tests for diagram-to-typescript code generation
description: diagram-to-typescript.ts (430 lines) generates TypeScript from UML diagrams but has no test file. Tests needed for: interface generation, abstract class generation, enum generation, class with inheritance, class with multiple implements, composition/aggregation field generation, import map generation, edge cases (empty class, class with no methods).
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at src/lib/lld/__tests__/codegen-typescript.test.ts or verify existing test at src/__tests__/lib/lld/codegen-typescript.test.ts
Tests cover all 4 stereotypes: class, interface, abstract, enum
Tests cover inheritance (extends), realization (implements)
Tests cover composition/aggregation field generation
Tests cover import map correctness
Tests verify generated code is syntactically valid TypeScript

title: Add text-based pattern key-differences comparison table
description: Team validation killed side-by-side SVG canvas (no auto-layout, cognitive overload). Replace with lightweight text-based comparison: dropdown to pick 2 patterns, shows a 3-row table (Intent, Structure, Key Tradeoff) plus links to load each pattern. Skeptic agent confirmed a static table does the job at 2 hours vs 2 weeks. Uses existing whenToUse/whenNotToUse fields from DesignPattern type.
acceptanceCriteria: Compare dropdown in sidebar allows selecting 2 patterns
Comparison shows text table: Intent, Structure Difference, Key Tradeoff
At least 6 commonly confused pairs have curated difference descriptions
Each pattern name in table is clickable to load it on canvas
Table renders in properties panel area, not a split canvas

title: Add difficulty indicators and learning path to design patterns
description: 20 patterns now have difficulty 1-5 field. Patterns browser shows flat list within categories. Add: difficulty level (1-5) to each DesignPattern, sort by difficulty within categories, show 'Recommended Next' links between patterns (e.g., 'You learned Strategy → Try State next'), and a 'Beginner Path' that highlights the 5 essential patterns to learn first.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pattern has a difficulty 1-5 field displayed as badge in sidebar
Patterns sorted by difficulty within each category
Properties panel shows 'Recommended Next' with 1-2 suggested patterns
A 'Beginner Path' filter in sidebar shows 5 essential patterns
Difficulty mapping is pedagogically sound (Singleton=1, Mediator=4, etc.)

title: Add code-based SOLID violation quiz with 25 questions
description: Skeptic agent recommended code-based input (not UML) — faster to author, more practical. Show TypeScript/Python code snippets (under 15 lines each) containing SOLID violations. User identifies which principle is violated from 5 options. Correct answer reveals explanation from existing SOLID_DEMOS.explanation + the before/after UML. Wrong answer shows hint and allows retry. Need 5 questions per principle (25 total). Use code excerpts, not the UML beforeClasses, as quiz input.
acceptanceCriteria: New bottom panel tab: 'SOLID Quiz'
Each question shows a code snippet (TS or Python, user selectable)
User selects which SOLID principle is violated from 5 options
Correct answer shows explanation + animates before/after UML from SOLID_DEMOS
Wrong answer shows targeted hint, allows retry
25 questions total (5 per principle), code under 15 lines each
Session score tracking with summary at end

title: Add LLD interview practice mode with timer, reveal, and self-assess checklist
description: Skeptic agent recommended: ship timer + requirements-only view + reveal reference + self-assess checklist NOW, defer automated grading indefinitely. LLDChallengeDefinition in lld-challenges.ts already has checklist field with grading criteria. Show requirements only, hide starter diagram, start timer, let user build. On submit/timeout, show reference solution side-by-side. User self-assesses against the existing checklist. No automated diff algorithm needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Practice mode toggle in Problems sidebar hides starter diagram
Timer visible with configurable duration (15/30/45/60 min)
User builds diagram from blank canvas using palette + editing
Submit/timeout reveals reference solution alongside user's diagram
Self-assessment checklist from lld-challenges.ts checklist field shown
User checks off items they covered; score = checked/total items
No automated structural diffing — human self-assessment only

title: Add static 'Pattern Finder' reference card to sidebar
description: Skeptic agent killed the decision engine (beginners can't answer the questions, experts don't need it). Replace with a static, always-visible reference card in the Patterns sidebar: two columns, 'I need to...' and 'Use this pattern.' One sentence per pattern using existing whenToUse fields. Example: 'I need to notify multiple objects when state changes → Observer.' No wizard, no questions, just a scannable reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Reference card visible in Patterns sidebar (collapsible section at top)
One row per pattern: scenario description + pattern name (clickable)
Clicking pattern name loads it on canvas
Scenario descriptions use plain language (not jargon)
All 15 patterns covered

title: Add progress tracking for patterns, SOLID, and problems
description: Zero progress tracking exists (Octalysis score: 0/5 for Accomplishment). Users have no sense of completion or mastery. Add: track which patterns viewed, which SOLID demos toggled both ways, which problems attempted. Show progress bar in sidebar header ('7/15 patterns explored'). Persist to localStorage. Show checkmarks on completed items.
acceptanceCriteria: Progress bar shows 'N/M explored' for each sidebar mode
Viewing a pattern for >5 seconds marks it as explored
Toggling SOLID demo both before→after marks it as completed
Loading a problem starter diagram marks it as attempted
Checkmark icon on explored/completed items in sidebar lists
Progress persists across page refreshes via localStorage
Reset progress button in settings

title: Add Mermaid syntax export for class diagrams
description: Research showed Mermaid has 87K GitHub stars and is native in GitHub/Notion/Confluence. Adding Mermaid export allows users to take their diagrams anywhere. Add 'Export as Mermaid' button in GeneratedCodePanel that converts current classes/relationships to Mermaid classDiagram syntax. This piggybacks on the massive Mermaid ecosystem for viral distribution.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Export as Mermaid' option alongside TypeScript/Python in GeneratedCodePanel
Generated Mermaid syntax is valid and renders correctly in Mermaid Live Editor
All UML relationship types mapped to Mermaid equivalents
Class stereotypes shown as annotations
Attributes and methods included in class definitions
Copy button works for Mermaid output

title: Add guided discovery walkthrough for Decorator pattern (validate format before scaling)
description: Skeptic agent recommended: build infra + ONE exemplary walkthrough (Decorator), measure completion rate, then decide whether to invest in 14 more. Build step-navigation infrastructure (step indicator, instruction panel, Next/Back, partial diagram state per step). Author 5-7 steps for Decorator: (1) Notifier class, (2) add email+SMS requirement, (3) try inheritance → class explosion, (4) introduce component interface, (5) apply Decorator, (6) compose decorators, (7) see full pattern. Each step has its own UMLClass[]/UMLRelationship[] partial state.
acceptanceCriteria: Discovery mode toggle appears on pattern view
Step navigation infrastructure: indicator, instruction panel, Next/Back buttons
Decorator walkthrough has 5-7 steps with canvas state + narrative per step
Each step renders partial UML diagram progressing toward full pattern
Final step shows complete Decorator pattern with summary
Completion rate trackable (for validation before scaling to more patterns)

title: Add empty state with recommended starting points
description: Current empty state at LLDModule.tsx:1158-1168 shows a faint PenTool icon and one line of text. Score: 3/10. Replace with an inspiring onboarding experience: show 3 recommended starting points ('Start with Singleton (easiest pattern)', 'Try the Parking Lot problem', 'Learn SRP — the most important SOLID principle'), a 'What do you want to learn?' quick-select, and a subtle animated diagram in the background.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state shows 3 clickable recommended starting points
Each recommendation has icon, title, one-line description, and difficulty badge
Clicking a recommendation loads it immediately
'What are you learning?' selector offers: Patterns, SOLID, Interview Prep
Empty state feels inviting, not empty (subtle background animation or illustration)
Recommendations are personalized once progress tracking exists (show unvisited items)

title: Add native select replacement with custom styled dropdowns
description: PatternBehavioralSimulator.tsx:338-349 and SequenceDiagramLatencyOverlay.tsx:206-219 use native browser <select> elements which render as unstyled OS defaults. Replace with custom styled dropdown components matching the platform design system using existing CSS variables. Both components already import motion/react for other animations.
acceptanceCriteria: Native <select> elements replaced with custom dropdown components
Dropdowns styled with platform CSS variables
Keyboard accessible (arrow keys, Enter, Escape)
Dropdown options show scenario descriptions
Visual appearance matches sidebar button styling

title: Add pattern recognition quiz ('Which pattern is this?')
description: Creative technique INVERT yielded: show a UML diagram, user names the pattern. Build quiz mode: show a pattern's class diagram WITHOUT its name, give 4 options, user picks. Uses existing DESIGN_PATTERNS data — just hide the pattern name and category badge during quiz. Randomize options from other patterns in same category for harder distractors. Track score per session.
acceptanceCriteria: Quiz mode accessible from Patterns sidebar section
Shows pattern UML diagram without name, category, or description
4 multiple-choice options from same or adjacent categories
Correct answer reveals pattern name + description
Wrong answer shows hint ('Look at the number of interfaces')
All 15 patterns appear in random order
Session score displayed at end

title: Add presentation mode for classroom/lecture use
description: Professor persona needs fullscreen diagrams with keyboard-only control for projectors. Add presentation mode: F key or button toggles fullscreen canvas, hides sidebar/properties/bottom panel, increases font sizes, arrow keys step through walkthrough steps or cycle patterns within a category. Exit with Escape. Uses existing canvas components at larger scale.
acceptanceCriteria: F key or toolbar button enters presentation mode
Fullscreen canvas: sidebar, properties panel, bottom panel hidden
Font sizes increased by 1.5x for projection readability
Arrow keys cycle through patterns within current category
Escape exits presentation mode, restores normal layout
Works with all 3 canvas types (class, sequence, state machine)

title: Add interview countdown study plan generator
description: Creative technique: 'first tool that generates a study plan from your interview date.' User enters interview date (e.g., 14 days away). System generates a day-by-day plan: Day 1-3 SOLID fundamentals, Day 4-7 key patterns (Observer, Strategy, Factory), Day 8-12 practice problems, Day 13-14 mock interview simulations. Uses existing content — just sequences it into a calendar view. Purely frontend calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field for interview date (date picker or days-from-now)
Generates day-by-day study plan from today to interview date
Plan allocates time across: SOLID (20%), Patterns (40%), Problems (30%), Review (10%)
Each day links to the specific pattern/problem/demo to study
Adjusts granularity: 7-day plan is condensed, 30-day plan is thorough
Shareable as text/markdown for note-taking

title: Add 'Pattern X-Ray' — paste code, detect which patterns it contains
description: Creative technique INVERT: 'detect patterns you already use unconsciously.' User pastes their own TypeScript/Python code. Existing code-to-diagram parser extracts classes/relationships. New analyzer scans for pattern signatures: class with single static getInstance() → Singleton, interface with multiple implementations → Strategy/Factory, class delegating to composed object with same interface → Decorator. Leverages existing parseTypeScriptCode/parsePythonCode.
acceptanceCriteria: New option in Code sidebar tab: 'Detect Patterns in Code'
User pastes code, parser extracts classes/relationships
Analyzer checks structural signatures against known patterns
Results show: detected pattern name + confidence + which classes match
Clicking a detected pattern highlights the matching classes on canvas
At least Singleton, Strategy, Observer, Factory Method, Decorator detectable

title: Add daily pattern challenge with streak counter
description: Engagement audit scored 0/5 for Avoidance drive. Add a daily challenge banner: each day features a random pattern to identify, a mini quiz question, or a 3-minute build challenge. Completing it increments a streak counter. Missing a day resets the streak. Uses existing pattern/quiz data. Streak stored in localStorage. Creates a reason to return daily.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily challenge banner at top of module (dismissible)
Challenge rotates daily (based on day-of-year mod content count)
Challenge types: identify pattern from UML, answer SOLID question, or build a pattern in 3 minutes
Completing challenge increments streak counter
Streak counter visible in sidebar header
Streak persists across sessions via localStorage
Missing a day resets streak to 0

title: Add screen reader mode with text-based diagram descriptions
description: Blind user persona has zero experience with current module (0 ARIA attributes). Add a 'Screen Reader Mode' toggle that replaces the SVG canvas with a structured text description: 'Observer Pattern: 4 classes. Subject (abstract): attributes [observers: Observer[]], methods [attach(), detach(), notify()]. Observer (interface): methods [update()]. ConcreteSubject extends Subject. ConcreteObserver implements Observer.' Uses existing class/relationship data, just renders as accessible HTML instead of SVG.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle in toolbar: 'Screen Reader Mode'
SVG canvas replaced with structured HTML text description
Each class listed with stereotype, attributes, methods, visibility
Relationships listed as sentences: 'ConcreteSubject inherits from Subject'
All content keyboard-navigable
aria-live region announces changes on pattern switch
Works with VoiceOver, NVDA, and JAWS

title: Add one-click video export of pattern animation via MediaRecorder
description: Content creator persona needs shareable video content. Add Record button that captures the canvas + any step-through/walkthrough animation as an MP4 using the MediaRecorder API + canvas.captureStream(). User clicks Record, steps through a pattern walkthrough or runs a simulation, clicks Stop, downloads video. Requires rendering SVG to canvas element for capture.
acceptanceCriteria: Record button appears in canvas toolbar
Click Record starts capturing canvas as video stream
Click Stop renders video and triggers download as .webm or .mp4
Captures all canvas interactions: drag, walkthrough steps, simulation
Video includes pattern title overlay
Works in Chrome and Firefox (MediaRecorder support)

title: Fix mobile bottom sheet not auto-dismissing after pattern selection
description: LIVE BROWSER TEST (April 2026, Chrome DevTools MCP, 375x812 mobile viewport): After clicking Observer in the mobile bottom sheet sidebar, the pattern loads behind the sheet but the sheet remains open covering the entire canvas. User cannot see the diagram. Pressing Escape does not dismiss. The bottom sheet dialog (a11y role='dialog' modal) at the module renderer level needs to auto-close when a sidebar item triggers a canvas update, or provide a clear dismiss gesture.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking any pattern/SOLID/problem/sequence/state-machine item auto-dismisses the mobile bottom sheet
Canvas is visible immediately after selection on mobile
User can re-open the sheet via a floating action button or swipe-up gesture
Works for all 7 sidebar modes

title: Fix duplicate 'PATTERN' in canvas title bar for pattern mode
description: LIVE BROWSER TEST: When Observer pattern is loaded, the canvas title bar shows 'OBSERVER PATTERN PATTERN' — the word PATTERN appears twice. Root cause: LLDModule.tsx:3344 computes canvasTitle as `activePattern.name + ' Pattern'` but LLDCanvas at line 1179 renders `{patternName} Pattern`. Either the hook adds 'Pattern' OR the canvas component does, not both. Fix: remove ' Pattern' suffix from one location.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas title shows 'OBSERVER PATTERN' not 'OBSERVER PATTERN PATTERN'
All 15 patterns show correct single-word title
Other canvas modes (SOLID, Problems, Sequence, State Machine) unaffected

title: Optimize UMLEdge with Map lookup instead of Array.find
description: UMLEdge uses classes.find(c => c.id === rel.source) per edge. Built a classById Map in LLDCanvas via useMemo and pass it to UMLEdge for O(1) lookups.
acceptanceCriteria: UMLEdge receives classById Map instead of classes array
classById Map is built via useMemo in LLDCanvas
Edge rendering behavior unchanged

title: Add error boundary around canvases
description: Wrap LLDCanvas, SequenceDiagramCanvas, StateMachineCanvas in an ErrorBoundary with a custom fallback showing 'Something went wrong' + 'Reset' button that calls clearAllModes().
acceptanceCriteria: All three canvas components are wrapped in ErrorBoundary
Fallback shows 'Something went wrong' message
Reset button calls clearAllModes to restore empty state
Error details logged to console

title: Show legend without pattern selected when classes exist on canvas
description: LLDCanvas legend was inside {patternName && (...)} so it didn't show when user added classes from Palette. Fixed to show legend whenever classes.length > 0.
acceptanceCriteria: Legend shows whenever classes exist on canvas
Title bar shows 'Class Diagram' when no specific pattern/demo/problem is active
Still shows pattern name when a pattern is selected

title: Share button with URL state encoding
description: Added share button to canvas toolbar. On click: encode current state as URL query params (?lld=pattern:observer etc.), copy to clipboard, show 'Link copied!' toast. Also parses URL params on mount to restore state.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button appears when a diagram is loaded
Clicking copies URL with ?lld= param to clipboard
Toast shows 'Link copied!' for 2 seconds
URL updates via replaceState on every selection change
Opening URL with ?lld= param restores the correct diagram

title: Deep-linkable URLs for all LLD modes
description: URL params work for ALL modes: patterns, SOLID, problems, sequence, state-machine. Invalid IDs fall back to empty state gracefully. Implemented together with LLD-140.
acceptanceCriteria: ?lld=pattern:<id> loads pattern
?lld=solid:<id> loads SOLID demo
?lld=problem:<id> loads problem
?lld=sequence:<id> loads sequence diagram
?lld=state-machine:<id> loads state machine
Invalid IDs show empty state without errors

title: Staggered entry animation for UML class boxes and edges
description: Wrapped UMLClassBox in motion.g with staggered spring animation (delay: index * 0.08). Edges fade in after all classes finish animating. Respects prefers-reduced-motion via matchMedia check.
acceptanceCriteria: UML class boxes animate in with staggered spring animation
Edges fade in after all classes have appeared
Animation skipped when prefers-reduced-motion is set
No layout shift during animation

title: Expand LLD problems from 10 to 33 to match awesome-low-level-design curriculum
description: LIVE RESEARCH (April 2026): awesome-low-level-design (23.3K GitHub stars) lists 33 LLD problems as the de facto interview prep curriculum. Architex has 10 in problems.ts and 25 in lld-problems-data.ts. Missing problems from the community standard include: Splitwise, Ride-Sharing (Uber), Movie Ticket Booking, Online Shopping (Amazon), Stock Brokerage, Music Streaming (Spotify), Food Delivery (Swiggy), CricInfo, Course Registration, Digital Wallet, Online Auction, Coffee Vending Machine, Social Network, Pub-Sub System. Add starter diagrams for the missing problems.
acceptanceCriteria: problems.ts contains 33 LLD problems matching awesome-low-level-design list
Each new problem has 3-5 starter UML classes with attributes/methods
Each has typed relationships, 4-5 requirements, and 3 hints
Difficulty levels align with awesome-low-level-design Easy/Medium/Hard categorization
All 33 appear in ProblemsBrowser sidebar
